decided in minutes.

FEASIBILITY INTELLIGENCE PLATFORM · FABRIC + POWER BI + COPILOT
FEDELIS
02 · THE QUESTION
03 / 12
The one question leadership keeps asking

Should we proceed
with this project?

Today, the answer takes
2–4 weeks
With Fabric + Copilot
Minutes
Why the weeks — where the time actually goes

Every project starts from zero

Copy last project's Excel, find-and-replace assumptions, hope nothing breaks.

One variable = long wait

Change selling price 5% — wait for hundreds of formulas to recalculate.

What-if questions stall

"Delay sales by 6 months?" — answering live is impossible.

Outputs are slides, not data

Every report is a hand-built PPT. Once printed, the numbers are frozen.

FEASIBILITY INTELLIGENCE PLATFORM · FABRIC + POWER BI + COPILOT
FEDELIS
03 · ARCHITECTURE
04 / 12
End-to-end · five layers · in one diagram

From data, all the way to decision.

Each layer removes work a person used to do. The handoffs are automatic.

Layer 01 · Sources
X
Excel
DB
ERP
C
CRM
$
Market
Where data lives today
→
Layer 02 · Process
Microsoft
Fabric
OneLake · storage
Data Factory · ingest
Warehouse · model
Cleans · combines · models
→
Layer 03 · Visualise
Power BI
Dashboard
Exec summary · KPIs
ROI · cost · margin
Scenarios
Risk indicators
What leadership sees
→
Layer 04 · Ask
Copilot
In Power BI
"Is Project X viable?"
"What if cost +10%?"
"Explain ROI drop"
Plain English Q&A
→
Layer 05 · Decide
C-Level
Decision
✓ Go
— Hold
✗ Reject
Same meeting · same morning

04 · DATA YOU HAVE
05 / 12
Step 1 · Fabric pulls every source into one place

The data you already have.

Nothing new to collect. Fabric reads where it already lives — and parks it in OneLake, your single shelf of truth.

X
Excel · QS & Finance
Cost · cashflow · feasibility
ERP · Accounting
Actuals · GL · vendor payments
CRM · Sales pipeline
Take-up rate · pipeline · leads
Market · External
Pricing indices · competitors
PDFs · Quotes & reports
Vendor quotes · approval letters
PULL · AUTO
no manual export · no double entry
Microsoft Fabric
OneLake
One shelf
one truth
Refreshes daily — no analyst, no rebuild.

TDC by category
Scenario margin
BEAR
12.4%
BASE
19.8%
BULL
26.4%
The Power BI dashboard leadership opens
Copilot
In Power BI · grounded
What if material cost increases by 10%?
CFO · just now
Margin drops from 19.8% to 16.2% (−3.6pp).
Net profit falls RM 41M.
Driver: construction is 41% of TDC. A 10% rise = RM 41M direct cost increase, no offsetting revenue.
19.8%
16.2%
BASE+10% COST
Copilot · 1 second ago
Should we still proceed?
Ask Copilot about this dashboard…
Compare projects Explain ROI Top risks
Copilot lives inside the Power BI report — same numbers, same source, answered on screen.

08 · THE PATH FORWARD
09 / 12
Start with the foundation, layer up one project at a time

Four steps, in order.

First 4 weeks: foundation + Feasibility dashboard. After that: extend to Cash Flow and Sales, then keep running.

The 4-week PoC
Weeks 1 – 2
Weeks 3 – 4
+ 3 mo / module
Ongoing
01 First
Build the
data foundation

We set up Microsoft Fabric and OneLake. Excel files only at this stage — your QS workbooks, finance cashflow, feasibility models.

X
Excel only
What you get
Excel data in one place, refreshed daily.
02 Then
Build the
Feasibility dashboard

Power BI dashboard on top of the foundation. Go / Hold / Reject answered in one click. Copilot turned on.

📊
Power BI live
What you get
Live Go/No-Go for every project.
03 Next
Add Cash Flow
& Sales modules

Extend the platform. Cash flow tracking, sales pipeline — and connect ERP, CRM and market data into the same foundation.

DB
+ ERP
C
+ CRM
$
+ Market
What you get
One platform, more decisions.
04 Always
Keep it
running

We watch the platform. Fix issues fast. Train new staff. Add features when you need them.

Ongoing support
Ongoing service
You focus. We maintain.
Why this order The database is built once and feeds every later module. Cash Flow and Sales don't need a second data setup — they plug straight into what's already there.
FEASIBILITY INTELLIGENCE PLATFORM · FABRIC + POWER BI + COPILOT
FEDELIS
09 · A 4-WEEK TEST RUN
10 / 12
Step one in detail — what happens, week by week

Try it on one project first.

No long contract. No big budget. Pick one project, give us 4 weeks. At the end, you decide whether to continue.

WEEK
01
WEEK
02
WEEK
03
WEEK
04
DAY 30
★
Listen

We sit with your QS, finance and IC team. Look at the real Excel files you use today.

End of week
We understand your numbers
Connect

We connect your Excel files into Fabric. Same numbers — just now in one place, refreshed daily.

End of week
Your data flows automatically
Build

We build the dashboard. Then we put it side-by-side with your current Excel — the numbers must match.

End of week
Dashboard works, numbers match
Hand over

Final review with CEO and CFO. Train your team. You can use it on Monday morning.

End of week
You own a working platform
Decide
Day 30

Continue to step 2 — or stop, no questions asked.

What we need from you
Not much.
1 project to use as the test 2 hours / week from your QS & finance lead Existing Excel files — no new data needed
FEASIBILITY INTELLIGENCE PLATFORM · FABRIC + POWER BI + COPILOT
FEDELIS
